_description: L-bend thermal absorber — a classic pipe stress teaching example (Peng & Peng Ch 5 style). 3m horizontal + 3m vertical carbon-steel pipe with anchors at both ends, 50 bar internal pressure, 20→120°C thermal expansion. The thermal growth of the horizontal leg pushes the corner, which is absorbed by bending in the vertical leg. Both sustained and expansion stress are moderate — the L-bend geometry is a natural flexibility.
